description: CSCL task types for teacher-centred activity generation. Includes argumentation patterns and broader collaborative patterns with compatibility metadata for alignment checking.

task_types.description: Position-based argumentation with evidence and rebuttals; roles for proposer, opposer, moderator.
task_types.pedagogical_goal: Develop evidence-based argumentation and critical response to counterclaims.

task_types.description: Groups compare and evaluate multiple sources of evidence; reach a justified conclusion.
task_types.pedagogical_goal: Compare evidence quality and synthesize across sources for collaborative argumentation.

task_types.description: Integrate multiple perspectives into a shared artifact; balance viewpoints and justify claims.
task_types.pedagogical_goal: Synthesize diverse perspectives into a coherent argumentative artifact.

task_types.description: Participants adopt roles to advance claims and counterclaims; negotiate and refine positions.
task_types.pedagogical_goal: Practice claim–counterclaim reasoning and role-based argumentation.

task_types.description: Each member becomes expert on one part; then teach peers and combine to complete a shared task.
task_types.pedagogical_goal: Distribute expertise and build interdependence through teaching and synthesis.

task_types.description: Groups analyse a scenario or case; identify issues, apply concepts, and propose solutions.
task_types.pedagogical_goal: Apply concepts to real situations and justify interpretations collaboratively.

task_types.description: Open-ended problem; groups define questions, gather information, and propose solutions.
task_types.pedagogical_goal: Develop problem-solving and self-directed learning through collaborative inquiry.

task_types.description: Individual think, then pair discussion, then share with class. Short structured cycle.
task_types.pedagogical_goal: Quick engagement and peer exchange before whole-class discussion.

task_types.description: Groups create artefacts; then rotate to view others’ work and leave feedback or compare.
task_types.pedagogical_goal: Expose students to multiple solutions and perspectives; peer feedback.

task_types.description: Inner circle discusses; outer circle observes and then swaps or feeds back.
task_types.pedagogical_goal: Structured whole-class discussion with clear roles and reflection.

task_types.description: Groups build a shared concept map linking ideas, evidence, and relationships.
task_types.pedagogical_goal: Make conceptual structure visible and negotiate meanings collaboratively.

task_types.description: Students review each other’s work against criteria; give feedback and optionally revise.
task_types.pedagogical_goal: Develop critical evaluation and use criteria; improve work through feedback.
